title: Boat Rental in Cabo Frio: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Cabo Frio, on Brazil's Costa do Sol, offers affordable boat rentals from Porto do Forno, with prices from $50 USD per person on shared tours to $400 USD for private vessels. BoatHire24 connects you with verified options for a day on the water.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cabo Frio?

Shared tours start at $50 USD per person, while private motorboats from $150 for half-day. Prices vary by season, with peak months December to March commanding a 20% premium. Catamarans for a full day cost from $600 USD, and crewed yachts from $1,200 USD. You can browse day charters in Cabo Frio on BoatHire24 for real-time pricing.

Where to set off in Cabo Frio

Porto do Forno is the main departure point, just east of the historic centre. Other launch sites include Marina Porto Búzios (20 km north), Praia das Dunas (for smaller boats), and Ilha do Japonês anchorage for dive trips. The Canal de Itajuru connects Lagoa de Araruama with the Atlantic, offering calm waters for beginners. Nearby towns Arraial do Cabo and Búzios add further charter bases. Most trips head to Ilha do Cabo Frio, 5 nautical miles offshore, known for clear water and sea turtles. You can also see more on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat (20, 25 ft), best for small groups or couples exploring Ilha do Japonês; up to 6 guests; from $150 USD half-day.
  2. Catamaran (30, 40 ft), ideal for parties or families; up to 12 guests; from $600 USD full-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (30, 40 ft), for a relaxed cruise on Lagoa de Araruama; up to 8 guests; from $350 USD half-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (40, 50 ft), includes skipper and fuel for a full-day trip to Ilha do Cabo Frio; up to 10 guests; from $1,200 USD.

For a fast and nimble option, check the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page on BoatHire24, a popular choice for two to four people seeking speed and ease of handling around the islands.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cabo Frio?

You do not need a Brazilian licence for a skippered charter, but bareboat rentals require an ICC or equivalent. The operator must confirm exact requirements. Most budget-minded visitors opt for skippered trips to avoid paperwork. A genuine local tip: in high season (December to March), the prevailing northeast wind can make the sea choppy by midday, so book morning departures for calmer conditions.

If you are hiring a bareboat, ask the marina to check your licence validity at least two weeks before arrival; popular models like the Lagoon 42 often require proof of experience.

Cost breakdown

  • Shared tour: from $50 USD per person for 3 hours, including snorkel gear.
  • Small motorboat half-day: $150, $250 USD for up to 6 guests, no licence needed with skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day: $600, $900 USD for up to 12 passengers, drinks often included.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: $1,200, $2,000 USD for a premium Azimut or Sunseeker, with waiter service.

At a glance

  • Price band: $50, $2,000 USD.
  • Peak months: December to March.
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full day.
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests.
  • Currency: US dollars.

Questions about boat rental in Cabo Frio

What is the best time for boat trips in Cabo Frio?

From April to November, the weather is mild with lighter winds, meaning smoother seas and lower prices compared to the summer peak.

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Cabo Frio?

Yes, but you must hold a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) and the rental company will verify it before release.

Are there budget group tours available?

Yes, shared schooner trips from Porto do Forno cost around $50 USD per person and visit Ilha do Cabo Frio and Praia do Farol.
